workshop_pitch: You selected Institutional Enforcer plus Grieving Zealot and got a villain whose favorite workplace protection is documentation. Their primary moral texture is flow sabotage. Their exposure lateral is Strategic Accountability Avoidance. Their core competency stack is Procedural Domination. Their sidekick is a person who keeps getting dragged into this who stays because they are genuinely loyal. Their formal training comes from The Institute of Smelling a Scam Before It Happens.
opening_image: By the time somebody names the monster correctly, the monster has already become a workflow.
dossier_summary: This build runs on uniforms inside a tribunal chamber. At the story level, it makes the machine legible through procedure. The primary moral texture is flow sabotage. The internal lie is simple: the rules are there for a reason. The exposure profile is strategic accountability avoidance, which operationalized into procedural domination. The local ecosystem includes a person who keeps getting dragged into this who handles emotional containment. Before any grand plan begins, the daily proof of rot is standing still on the left side of the escalator, but the full civilian rot profile also includes trapping customer support inside a chatbot with no human exit, abandoning your cart across the aisle while you backtrack for one item, taking your charger and acting confused about it.
public_facing_bio: Publicly, this figure presents as polished, necessary, and strangely inevitable. Privately, they are a pure engine of flow sabotage shaped by strategic accountability avoidance and confident enough to treat procedural sadism like a normal leadership behavior. The person who keeps getting dragged into this nearby makes the whole thing feel alarmingly maintainable.
internal_memo: Internal note: continue protecting the principal from low-level reputation damage tied to standing still on the left side of the escalator. Pattern review now includes trapping customer support inside a chatbot with no human exit, abandoning your cart across the aisle while you backtrack for one item, taking your charger and acting confused about it. Their current strengths remain procedural sadism, selective helplessness, permissions gating. The person who keeps getting dragged into this remains responsible for emotional containment and is currently genuinely loyal. Do not mention the Efficiency Consultant Liaison Office matter in writing.
signature_scene: The villain stages a scene inside the tribunal chamber where documentation briefly passes for order until the room notices too late that the real agenda is this: turns private loss into public doctrine. The person who keeps getting dragged into this quietly handles emotional containment in the background, which is why the whole thing almost works. Somewhere in the room, somebody realizes too late that the sidekick is actively making things worse and folds if shown receipts.
fake_trailer: In a world shaped by corporate satire, one institutional enforcer build will learn that the institution must reveal its violence to preserve itself and thereby loses legitimacy. Exposure: Strategic Accountability Avoidance. Core competencies: procedural sadism, selective helplessness. Sidekick: person who keeps getting dragged into this on emotional containment. Somebody is definitely standing still on the left side of the escalator in act one, but the real warning is that the rot profile also includes trapping customer support inside a chatbot with no human exit, abandoning your cart across the aisle while you backtrack for one item, taking your charger and acting confused about it.
sequel_slop: Franchise extension: somebody rebuilds the same logic with fresher branding, and the receipts get harder to kill. The petty tell survives too, because evil this scalable still finds time for standing still on the left side of the escalator. The wider rot profile survives too: trapping customer support inside a chatbot with no human exit, abandoning your cart across the aisle while you backtrack for one item, taking your charger and acting confused about it. So does the sidekick ecosystem: another person who keeps getting dragged into this arrives, still genuinely loyal, still handling emotional containment.
sample_lines: If standing still on the left side of the escalator bothers you, wait until you hear the rest of my rot profile. Then meet my person who keeps getting dragged into this
My core competencies include procedural sadism, and my person who keeps getting dragged into this handles emotional containment.
If this sounds compassionate, you are already inside the problem.
hooks: The real threat is not violence but the institutional permission structure surrounding it.

Even before the grand scheme, the villain was already the kind of person who would be standing still on the left side of the escalator, and the wider rot profile includes trapping customer support inside a chatbot with no human exit, abandoning your cart across the aisle while you backtrack for one item, taking your charger and acting confused about it.
The audience trusts the build less the moment they learn even the person who keeps getting dragged into this is hanging around because they are genuinely loyal.
